prompt: Inside the Polymarket '2026 FIFA World Cup Winner' event (60 contenders, July 20 final), rank the top 10 contenders by 24-hour trading volume and chart each contender's volume so I can see which teams the market is actually trading right now.
expectedReason: homepage showcase categorical charts need multi-row comparisons
responsePreview:
In the '2026 FIFA World Cup Winner' event on Polymarket, the market is currently seeing high activity across a mix of favorites and long-shot contenders. 

As of May 2, 2026, here are the top 10 contenders ranked by 24-hour trading volume:

| Rank | Contender | 24-Hour Volume | Implied Probability | Price |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 1 | Uzbekistan | $36,575,508.93 | 0.3% | $0.0025 |
| 2 | Curaçao | $33,659,035.57 | 0.3% | $0.0025 |
| 3 | USA | $30,256,893.37 | 1.6% | $0.0155 |
| 4 | New Zealand | $26,868,699.52 | 0.3% | $0.0025 |
| 5 | Saudi Arabia | $24,916,783.91 | 0.3% | $0.0025 |
| 6 | South Africa | $24,805,369.46 | 0.3% | $0.0025 |
| 7 | Jordan | $23,662,448.42 | 0.3% | $0.0025 |
| 8 | France | $22,790,070.36 | 16.4% | $0.1645 |
| 9 | Iran | $22,214,688.71 | 0.1% | $0.0015 |
| 10 | Qatar | $22,164,793.72 | 0.3% | $0.0025 |

The accompanying bar chart illustrates the significant trading interest in Uzbekistan and Curaçao, which currently lead the market in volume despite their very low implied probabilities. Among the top-tier favorites, France remains the most heavily traded, while the USA also sees substantial activity compared to other host nations.
